text.title: State Management Decision Tree and Performance
text.summary: Routing state to the right tool (TanStack Query, React Hook Form, Zustand, Context, useState) and applying memoization only where it pays off.

rule.statement: Route state to the correct layer: server state to TanStack Query, form state to React Hook Form, global UI state to Zustand/Jotai, component-local state to useState/useReducer.
rule.rationale: Each state type has different caching, synchronization, and invalidation needs; mixing them in one store produces accidental coupling and stale data bugs.

list.item: Server state (API data) → TanStack Query / SWR
Global UI state (theme, sidebar) → Zustand (simple) / Jotai (atomic)
Cross-component state in same feature → Context + useReducer

rule.statement: Memoize only after profiling confirms a re-render problem; default to no memoization for simple computations.
rule.rationale: useMemo and useCallback add overhead (allocation + comparison); blindly applying them for every value creates more work, not less.

antipattern.description: Passing inline objects or arrays as JSX props: <Comp style={{ color: 'red' }} />
antipattern.reason: A new object is created on every render, breaking React.memo equality checks and causing unnecessary child re-renders.
